case_title: THELMA FLORES-ARROYO VS. NORMAN YUNG DMD et al

CASE MANAGEMENT STATEMENT FILED BY CROSS COMPLAINANT YUNG DMD, NORMAN DR. JUSTIN TIN AND DR. NORMAN YUNG DENTAL CORPORATION (ERRONEOUSLY SUED AS DR. NORMAN YUNG AND DR. JUSTIN TIN DENTAL CORPORATION) DBA MISSION STREET DENTAL JURY DEMANDED, ESTIMATED TIME FOR TRIAL: 4.0 DAYS
REPLY TO DEFT NORMAN YUNG'S OPPOS. TO MTN TO COMPEL FILED BY PLAINTIFF FLORES-ARROYO, THELMA

DISCOVERY 302, PARTIES STIPULATE THAT THIS MOTION MAY BE HEARD BY JUDGE PRO TEM. MATTER ARGUED. AT CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT RULES AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ORDER COMPELLING DEFENDANTS RESPONSES IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT DR. NORMAN YUNG MUST PROVIDE ANSWERS WITH VERIFICATIONS BY JANUARY 22, 2013. PLAINTIFF AWARDED $630.00 ATTORNEY FEES. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ORDER COMPELLING FURTHER RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFF'S FIRST SET OF FORM INTERROGATORIES IS GRANTED. FURTHER ANSWERS TO BE PROVIDED WITHIN 15 DAYS OF ENTRY OF ORDER. PLAINTIFF AWARDED $1,000.OO ATTORNEY FEES. COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF TO SUBMIT PROPOSED ORDER. JUDGE PRO TEM WILLIAM SHAPIRO, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED.
PANEL OF ARBITRATORS ASSIGNED. REJECTION DATE FEB-21-2013.
PLAINTIFF(S) REJECTED PROPOSED ARBITRATOR SANDRA J. SHAPIRO.
OFFER AND ACCEPTANCE TO COMPROMISE [CCP 998] FILED BY DEFENDANT YUNG DMD, NORMAN

MASTER COURT CALENDAR SET FOR AUG-25-2014 CONTINUED TO MASTER CALENDAR COURT (CONTINUED) ON AUG-26-2014 AT 9:30 AM IN 206 ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION. JUDGE: CYNTHIA M. LEE; CLERK: JIROQUE; COURT REPORTER: MATTER NOT REPORTED. (206)

REMOVED FROM COURT TRIAL CALENDAR ON AUG-26-2014 IN DEPT. 502 THE COURT HEARS ARGUMENT AND THE COURT HEREBY DISMISSES THE COMPLAINT BASED ON LACK OF JURISDICTION OVER ANY DEFENDANT. THE COURT FINDS THERE IS NO JURISDICTION IN THIS ACTION OVER ANY PARTY. THE PLAINTIFF SERVED THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT ON DR. NORMAL YUNG, DR. JUSTIN TIN DENTAL CORPORATION AND MISSION STREET DENTAL ON MAY 7, 2012 AND SUBSEQUENTLY FILED A PROOF OF SERVICE. THIS CORPORATION WAS DISSOLVED ON DECEMBER 31, 2011. (SEE EXHIBIT 3 AND EXHIBIT 4 TO DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO DISMISS.) PLAINTIFF DID NOT USE THE PROCESS FOR SERVING DISSOLVED CORPORATIONS. (SEE CCP 416.20.) BEFORE THE COURT ANNOUNCED HIS FINAL DECISION, COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF REQUESTED LEAVE TO AMEND PURSUANT TO CCP 473. THE COURT DENIES THE MOTION ON THE GROUNDS THAT IT COMES TOO LATE. ACCORDINGLY, THE COURT GRANTS JUDGMENT AGAINST THELMA FLORES-ARROYO ON THE GROUND THAT THERE IS NO JURISDICTION SUPPORTING HER ACTION. JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON, II; CLERK: R. GOULDING; NOT REPORTED
